meta.description: Structured rules for BaZi chart calculation, Ten Gods determination, Five Elements relationships, New Age terminology mapping, and common pattern templates.

heavenlyStems.description: The 10 Heavenly Stems (天干 / Tian Gan) — the explicit energies visible on the surface of a BaZi chart.

甲.englishAlias: Yang Wood — the towering tree, pioneer spirit

乙.englishAlias: Yin Wood — the vine, graceful persistence

丙.englishAlias: Yang Fire — the sun, radiant leader

丁.englishAlias: Yin Fire — the candle flame, inner warmth

戊.englishAlias: Yang Earth — the mountain, steadfast protector

己.englishAlias: Yin Earth — the fertile soil, nurturing sustainer

庚.englishAlias: Yang Metal — the sword, decisive and principled

辛.englishAlias: Yin Metal — the jewel, refined and discerning

壬.englishAlias: Yang Water — the ocean, visionary and expansive

癸.englishAlias: Yin Water — the mist, deep intuition

earthlyBranches.description: The 12 Earthly Branches (地支 / Di Zhi) — representing time periods, hidden stems, and relationship dynamics.

fiveElements.description: The Five Elements (五行 / Wu Xing) and their relationships.

generatingCycle.description: The nurturing cycle: each element generates (feeds) the next.
sequence: Wood → Fire → Earth → Metal → Water → Wood

rules.Wood feeds Fire: Wood burns to create Fire — growth fuels passion
rules.Fire creates Earth: Fire produces ash, enriching Earth — passion grounds into stability
rules.Earth bears Metal: Earth compresses to form Metal — stability crystallizes into structure
rules.Metal enriches Water: Metal condenses moisture into Water — structure channels wisdom
rules.Water nourishes Wood: Water enables Wood to grow — wisdom feeds vision

controllingCycle.description: The balancing cycle: each element controls (restrains) another — not destruction, but regulation.
sequence: Wood controls Earth → Earth controls Water → Water controls Fire → Fire controls Metal → Metal controls Wood

rules.Wood restrains Earth: Tree roots stabilize soil — vision gives shape to practicality
rules.Earth contains Water: Riverbanks guide water flow — grounding channels emotion
rules.Water regulates Fire: Water keeps fire from raging — wisdom tempers passion
rules.Fire refines Metal: Fire melts and shapes metal — passion transforms structure
rules.Metal prunes Wood: Metal tools trim excess growth — discernment focuses vision

Wood.growthEdge: Can overextend; may struggle with follow-through; needs Metal to focus
Wood.lifeDimensions: Vision, growth, new beginnings, education, expansion

Fire.growthEdge: Can burn out; may overwhelm others; needs Water for sustainable warmth
Fire.lifeDimensions: Passion, expression, joy, social connection, visibility

Earth.growthEdge: Can become stuck or overly cautious; needs Wood to inspire movement

Metal.growthEdge: Can become rigid or overly critical; needs Fire to soften and warm

Water.growthEdge: Can become isolated or overwhelmed by emotion; needs Earth to anchor
Water.lifeDimensions: Wisdom, intuition, communication, flow, inner world

tenGodsRules.description: How to determine the Ten Gods (十神 / Shi Shen) from the Day Master. Given a Day Master stem D and another stem S, determine the Ten God relationship based on (1) Five Element relationship and (2) polarity match.

relationshipLogic.howToRead: Consider the Day Master as 'Self'. The element the Day Master generates is 'Output' (child). The element the Day Master controls is 'Wealth' (resource). The element that controls the Day Master is 'Officer' (authority). The element that generates the Day Master is 'Resource' (support).

elementRelationships.outputElements: The element generated BY the Day Master (e.g., Wood generates Fire — so if Day Master is Wood, Fire stems = Output gods)
elementRelationships.wealthElements: The element controlled BY the Day Master (e.g., Wood controls Earth — so if Day Master is Wood, Earth stems = Wealth gods)
elementRelationships.officerElements: The element that controls the Day Master (e.g., Metal controls Wood — so if Day Master is Wood, Metal stems = Officer gods)
elementRelationships.resourceElements: The element that generates the Day Master (e.g., Water generates Wood — so if Day Master is Wood, Water stems = Resource gods)

terminologyMapping.description: New Age / Western-friendly terminology mapping. Left column is the original Eastern concept; right column is the recommended English expression for Destiny Code reports. These replacements ensure cultural accessibility without losing meaning.

mappings.recommended: Energy Blueprint (or 'BaZi Energy Blueprint' on first mention)
Energy Codes / Primary Energies
Life Pillars / Time Anchors
Elemental Energies / Five Elemental Forces
Core Self Element / Your Signature Energy
Your Core Pillar — representing your essential self and partnership patterns
Your Ancestral & Legacy Pillar — early-life influences and outward-facing energy
Your Career & Action Pillar — how you engage with the world
Your Inner Compass Pillar — private self, later-life direction, inner world
Creative Flow — your natural talent and joy-expression channel
Trailblazer Talent — your innovative, boundary-pushing brilliance
Steady Resources — your grounded approach to building stability
Opportunity Magnet — your flair for spotting and seizing possibilities
Inner Compass — your integrity, discipline, and natural authority
Intensity Channel — your powerful drive that, when mastered, becomes charismatic leadership
Wisdom Anchor — your source of inner knowing, calm, and perspective
Unconventional Wisdom — your unique lens, intuition, and spiritual receptivity
Self-Reliance Core — your strong sense of self and independent spirit
Connection Catalyst — your social magnetism and collaborative drive
Supportive Element — the energy that brings you into flow
Growth Catalyst — the energy that challenges you to evolve
Energy Configuration / Blueprint Architecture
Strong Core — you carry abundant self-energy; you're naturally resilient
Responsive Core — you're highly attuned to your environment; your strength is adaptability
Polarity — the receptive (Yin) and active (Yang) dimensions of your energy
Nurturing Cycle & Balancing Cycle — the rhythms of elemental interaction
Dynamic Interactions — energetic dialogues between elements that create texture and growth
Surface Energy — visible traits and conscious patterns
Underlying Current — deeper drives and subconscious patterns

mappings.eastern: 长生十二宫 (12 Stages of Life Cycle)
mappings.recommended: Energy Lifecycle — where each element is in its natural rhythm of expression
Life Season — the overarching energetic theme of a decade (V2 feature)
Annual Current — the year's energetic theme (V2 feature)
Elemental Alignments — when stems harmonize to create new expressions

patternTemplates.description: Common BaZi chart patterns (格局 / Ge Ju) and their recommended interpretation templates. Each pattern comes with a personality profile and a 'growth edge' framing.

食神生财.name: Creative Flow Generating Resources (Shi Shen Sheng Cai)
食神生财.description: Eating God produces Wealth — creative talent leads to material and personal abundance.
食神生财.personality: You possess a rare combination: natural creative talent paired with an instinct for turning that creativity into tangible results. This is the energy of the artist-entrepreneur — someone whose joy and self-expression naturally attracts resources and opportunities. You're likely someone who doesn't feel like you're 'working' when you're in your flow state.
食神生财.growthEdge: The shadow side of this abundant creative energy is a tendency toward overindulgence or scattering your gifts across too many projects. Your growth invitation is to develop the Metal discernment to focus your creative fire — learning that saying 'no' to some possibilities deepens the ones you say 'yes' to.
careerDirections: Any role where creative output translates to value

伤官配印.name: Trailblazer Tempered by Wisdom (Shang Guan Pei Yin)
伤官配印.description: Hurting Officer paired with Resource — rebellious brilliance balanced by wisdom and structure.
伤官配印.personality: You carry one of the most intellectually gifted configurations: a mind that naturally questions everything (Hurting Officer) combined with a deep well of wisdom and discernment (Resource). You see what others miss. Your ideas don't just color outside the lines — they redraw the entire canvas. This is the energy of the visionary reformer.
伤官配印.growthEdge: Your brilliance can sometimes create distance — others may struggle to keep up, or may feel intimidated by your sharpness. Your growth invitation is to wrap your insights in warmth. The wisdom (Resource) in your chart is the bridge: when you lead with compassion, your ideas become movements.

正官正印.name: Integrity Anchored in Wisdom (Zheng Guan Zheng Yin)
正官正印.description: Direct Officer paired with Direct Resource — a classic configuration of integrity, wisdom, and steady leadership.
正官正印.personality: Your blueprint carries an inherently trustworthy architecture. The Direct Officer gives you a natural sense of duty and an internal moral compass that others instinctively respect. Paired with Direct Resource, you don't just know what's right — you understand why. You are someone who leads not through force but through the quiet authority of character.
正官正印.growthEdge: The shadow of this configuration is rigidity — a tendency to hold yourself (and others) to standards that leave little room for the beautiful messiness of being human. Your growth invitation is to soften your inner critic and trust that integrity doesn't require perfection.

财官双美.name: Resources and Authority in Harmony (Cai Guan Shuang Mei)
财官双美.description: Both Wealth and Officer are strong and well-balanced — indicating capacity for both building resources and holding responsibility.
财官双美.personality: You have a rare energetic balance between the drive to build (Wealth) and the capacity to lead (Officer). This is a blueprint that can hold both the vision and the execution — the strategy and the people. You're likely someone who naturally rises to positions of trust, not because you chase them, but because your energy signals reliability and competence.
财官双美.growthEdge: With both Wealth and Officer strong, the risk is over-identification with external achievement. Your growth invitation is to remember that your worth is inherent, not earned. The most important resource to build is your inner peace; the most important authority to hold is over your own boundaries.
careerDirections: High-responsibility roles requiring both vision and execution

杀印相生.name: Intensity Transmuted into Wisdom (Sha Yin Xiang Sheng)
杀印相生.description: Seven Killings generating Resource — powerful drive is channeled through wisdom and knowledge. One of the most transformative configurations.
杀印相生.personality: You carry one of the most potent and transformative energy signatures in BaZi. The Seven Killings gives you an intensity and drive that most people can't match — a kind of internal pressure that, when harnessed, becomes extraordinary resilience and leadership. Because it feeds your Resource star, this intensity doesn't just push you; it makes you wiser. You grow through challenges that would break others.
杀印相生.growthEdge: The Seven Killings energy, left unchanneled, can manifest as internal restlessness, anxiety, or a sense of always being 'under pressure.' Your life's curriculum is to learn to channel this intensity constructively. Physical discipline, structured learning, and meaningful challenges are your alchemical tools. You don't need less intensity — you need the right containers for it.
careerDirections: Any role requiring grace under pressure

从格.name: Following Pattern (Cong Ge)
从格.genericDescription: When the Day Master is extremely weak and the chart is dominated by one or two elements, the person's energy follows the dominant flow rather than standing as an independent force.
从格.personality: Your chart follows a unique architecture where one element so dominates the landscape that your energy naturally aligns with it rather than standing apart. This gives you an almost chameleon-like adaptability — you can thrive in environments that would overwhelm others because you don't resist the current; you flow with it.
从格.growthEdge: The invitation here is to discern when 'going with the flow' serves your highest good and when it becomes a pattern of self-abandonment. Your adaptability is a superpower; your challenge is to wield it consciously, not reflexively.
从格.note: This is a broad category that requires specific sub-pattern identification (从财/从杀/从儿/从强) for detailed interpretation.

pillarMeanings.description: What each of the Four Pillars represents in a person's life. Used to contextualize Ten Gods based on their pillar location.

yearPillar.name: Ancestral & Legacy Pillar
yearPillar.timeRange: Early childhood (0-16), ancestral influences, outward appearance to society, grandparents
yearPillar.interpretationContext: Ten Gods here speak to inherited patterns, the energy you present to the outside world, and your relationship with tradition and authority.

monthPillar.name: Career & Action Pillar
monthPillar.timeRange: Young adulthood (17-32), career development, parents (especially father), work ethic
monthPillar.interpretationContext: Ten Gods here speak to how you engage with work, your professional identity, and the energy you bring to pursuing goals.

dayPillar.name: Core Self & Partnership Pillar
dayPillar.timeRange: Mid-life (33-48), self-identity, marriage/partnership, the 'spouse palace' (日支)
dayPillar.interpretationContext: The Day Stem IS the Day Master — it represents your essential self. The Day Branch represents your inner world and partnership patterns.

hourPillar.name: Inner Compass & Legacy Pillar
hourPillar.timeRange: Later life (49+), children, inner thoughts, creative expression, dreams, legacy
hourPillar.interpretationContext: Ten Gods here speak to your private self, your relationship with the next generation, and the mark you leave on the world.

usefulGodsLogic.description: How to determine Useful Gods (用神 / Yong Shen) — the elements that bring balance and support to the chart.
usefulGodsLogic.generalPrinciple: A BaZi chart is balanced when the Day Master is neither too strong nor too weak. If the Day Master is strong, elements that output (child), control (wealth), or restrain (officer) the Day Master become favorable. If the Day Master is weak, elements that support (resource) or are the same as (friend/rob wealth) the Day Master become favorable.

dayMasterStrong.description: When the Day Master is strong (身强), the chart needs release and channeling.
favorableActions: Officer (direct energy through discipline and structure)
unfavorableActions: Resource (adds more support to an already-strong self)
Friend/Rob Wealth (amplifies the oversupply of self-energy)

dayMasterWeak.description: When the Day Master is weak (身弱), the chart needs support and nourishment.
unfavorableActions: Wealth (taxes limited energy to pursue resources)

dayMasterBalanced.description: When the Day Master is balanced, the focus is on maintaining harmony.
favorableActions: The element that facilitates smooth flow between the strongest and weakest energies in the chart

dayMasterStrengthAssessment.description: Quick assessment rules for determining Day Master strength (身强/身弱). This is a simplified heuristic; complete assessment requires full chart analysis.
factorsThatStrengthen: Day Master stem is born in season (e.g., Wood DM in spring months: 寅卯辰)
Many Friend (比肩) or Rob Wealth (劫财) Ten Gods in the chart
Strong Direct/Indirect Resource (正印/偏印) supporting the Day Master
Day Master sits on a branch that generates it (e.g., Wood DM on 子/亥 Water branch)
factorsThatWeaken: Day Master is out of season (e.g., Wood DM in autumn months: 申酉戌)
Many Output Ten Gods (Eating God / Hurting Officer) draining the Day Master
Strong Officer gods (Direct Officer / Seven Killings) controlling the Day Master
Strong Wealth gods (Direct/Indirect Wealth) that the Day Master must control
